p.strong: Proof: Linear layers without activation collapse into a single linear transformation.

div.p: Depth without non-linearity is a computational waste. A 1,000-layer network without activation functions has the exact same representational power as a single-layer perceptron. The activation function is the "engine" that enables deep learning by allowing the model to learn complex, non-planar boundaries in high-dimensional space.
If we stack linear layers without non-linearities:
Conclusion: Two layers are mathematically identical to one single layer.
With an activation function $\sigma$ (e.g., ReLU, Tanh):
Substituting results in: $y = W_2 \sigma(W_1 x + b_1) + b_2$

ul.li: Each layer creates a unique feature transformation
Result: Neural network can approximate any function.
